preamble: The shift from an empty position to the voiced dental fricative [ð] is a specialized form of epenthesis. It typically occurs to break a hiatus between vowels or as a result of the intervocalic lenition of an underlying dental stop.

examples.note: Phonetic epenthesis of the fricative between vowels (historically lenition of /d/).
Phonetic reinforcement of the dental onset in rapid speech.

sources: Penny: A History of the Spanish Language
